info.title: hoprd-api
info.description: API enabling developers to interact with a hoprd node programatically through HTTP REST API.

get.summary: Get node's native addresses.

Get node's and associated Safe's HOPR and native balances as the allowance for HOPR
tokens to be drawn by HoprChannels from Safe.
get.description:
HOPR tokens from the Safe balance are used to fund the payment channels between this
node and other nodes on the network.
NATIVE balance of the Node is used to pay for the gas fees for the blockchain.

200.description: The node's HOPR and Safe balances

post.summary: Withdraw funds from this node to the ethereum wallet address.
post.description: Withdraw funds from this node to the ethereum wallet address

requestBody.description: Request body for the withdraw endpoint

200.description: The node's funds have been withdrawn

get.summary:
Lists channels opened to/from this node. Alternatively, it can print all
the channels in the network as this node sees them.
get.description: List channels opened to/from this node. Alternatively, it can print all the channels in the network as this node sees them.

parameters.name: includingClosed
parameters.description: Should be the closed channels included?

parameters.name: fullTopology
parameters.description: Should all channels (not only the ones concerning this node) be enumerated?

post.summary: Opens a channel to the given on-chain address with the given initial stake of HOPR tokens.
post.description: Opens a channel to the given on-chain address with the given initial stake of HOPR tokens.

requestBody.description: Open channel request specification: on-chain address of the counterparty and the initial HOPR token stake.

400.description: Invalid counterparty address or stake amount.

403.description: Failed to open the channel because of insufficient HOPR balance or allowance.

409.description: Failed to open the channel because the channel between these nodes already exists.

get.summary: Returns information about the channel with the given counterparty address in the given direction.
get.description: Returns information about the channel with the given counterparty address. Use the `direction` query parameter to choose between the outgoing (this node → counterparty, default) and incoming (counterparty → this node) channel.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Direction of the channel relative to this node. Defaults to `outgoing`.

delete.summary: Closes the channel with the given counterparty in the given direction.
delete.description: Closes the channel with the given counterparty. Use the `direction` query parameter to choose between the outgoing (this node → counterparty, default) and incoming (counterparty → this node) channel.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Direction of the channel relative to this node. Defaults to `outgoing`.

post.summary: Funds the outgoing channel to the given counterparty with the given amount of HOPR tokens.
post.description: Funds the outgoing channel to the given counterparty with the given amount of HOPR tokens.

requestBody.description: Specifies the amount of HOPR tokens to fund a channel with.

400.description: Invalid counterparty address or request body.

403.description: Failed to fund the channel because of insufficient HOPR balance or allowance.

get.summary: Lists all announced peers.
get.description: List all announced peers

get.summary: Lists peers with immediate observation data from the network graph.
get.description: List connected peers with immediate observation data from the network graph

get.summary: Returns the network graph in DOT (Graphviz) format.
get.description: Get the network graph in DOT (Graphviz) format

parameters.name: reachableOnly
parameters.description:
When true, only include edges reachable from this node via directed
traversal. Disconnected subgraphs that cannot be routed through are excluded.

200.description: DOT representation of the network graph

get.summary: Gets the current ticket price.
get.description: Get the current ticket price

get.summary: Gets the current minimum incoming ticket winning probability defined by the network.
get.description: Get the current minimum incoming ticket winning probability defined by the network

200.description: Minimum incoming ticket winning probability defined by the network

get.summary: Get the configuration of the running node.
get.description: Get the configuration of the running node

get.summary: Get information about this HOPR Node.
get.description: Get information about this HOPR Node

get.summary: Get the aggregated status of this HOPR node and its individual components.
get.description: Get the aggregated status of this HOPR node and its individual components

get.summary: Get the release version of the running node.
get.description: Get the release version of the running node

get.summary: Returns comprehensive information about the given peer.
get.description:
Includes announced and observed multiaddresses, QoS observation data from the
network graph, and the state of any channels between this node and the peer.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: On-chain address of the requested peer

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: On-chain address of the requested peer

404.description: Peer address not found in the network.

get.description: Gets configuration of an existing active session.

404.description: Given session ID does not refer to an existing Session

post.description: Updates configuration of an existing active session.

requestBody.description: Allows updating of several parameters of an existing active session.

404.description: Given session ID does not refer to an existing Session

get.summary: Lists existing Session listeners for the given IP protocol.
get.description: Lists existing Session listeners for the given IP protocol.

200.description: Opened session listeners for the given IP protocol.

post.summary:
Creates a new client session returning the given session listening host and port over TCP or UDP.
If no listening port is given in the request, the socket will be bound to a random free
port and returned in the response.
Different capabilities can be configured for the session, such as data segmentation or
retransmission.
post.description: Creates a new client HOPR session that will start listening on a dedicated port. Once the port is bound, it is possible to use the socket for bidirectional read and write communication.

requestBody.description: Creates a new client HOPR session that will start listening on a dedicated port. Once the port is bound, it is possible to use the socket for bidirectional read and write communication.

200.description: Successfully created a new client session.

409.description: Listening address and port already in use.

delete.summary:
Closes an existing Session listener.
The listener must've been previously created and bound for the given IP protocol.
Once a listener is closed, no more socket connections can be made to it.
If the passed port number is 0, listeners on all ports of the given listening IP and protocol
will be closed.

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: Listening IP address of the Session.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: Session port used for the listener.

post.description: Starts redeeming tickets. When a counterparty address is specified, only tickets from that counterparty are redeemed.

requestBody.description: Optional counterparty address to scope ticket redemption.

400.description: Invalid request body or malformed JSON.

get.summary: Returns current complete statistics on tickets.
get.description: Returns current complete statistics on tickets.

200.description: Tickets statistics fetched successfully. Check schema for description of every field in the statistics.

get.summary: Check whether the node is eligible in the network.
get.description: Check whether the node is eligible in the network

200.description: The node is allowed in the network

412.description: The node is not allowed in the network

get.summary: Check whether the node is **healthy**.
get.description: Check whether the node is healthy

get.summary: Retrieve Prometheus metrics from the running node.
get.description: Retrieve Prometheus metrics from the running node

get.summary: Check whether the node is **ready** to accept connections.
get.description: Check whether the node is ready to accept connections

200.description: The node is ready to accept connections

412.description: The node is not ready to accept connections

get.summary: Check whether the node is started.
get.description: Check whether the node is started

200.description: The node is started and running

412.description: The node is not started and running

AccountBalancesResponse.description: Contains all node's and safe's related balances.

AnnouncedPeerResponse.description: A peer that has been announced.

AnnouncementOriginResponse.description: How a peer announcement was discovered.

ApiError.description: Standardized error response for the API

ChannelDirection.description: Direction of a channel relative to this node.

ChannelDirectionQuery.description: Query parameters selecting which channel (incoming or outgoing) to address.

oneOf.description: Direction of the channel relative to this node. Defaults to `outgoing`.

ChannelInfoResponse.description: General information about a channel state.

ChannelTicket.description: Represents a ticket in a channel.

fullTopology.description: Should all channels (not only the ones concerning this node) be enumerated?

includingClosed.description: Should be the closed channels included?

CloseChannelResponse.description: Status of the channel after a close operation.

receipt.description: Receipt for the channel close transaction.

ConnectedPeerResponse.description: Immediate observation data for a connected peer.

averageLatency.description: Average latency in milliseconds, if available.

lastUpdate.description: Epoch milliseconds of the last observation update.

FundBodyRequest.description: Specifies the amount of HOPR tokens to fund a channel with.

amount.description: Amount of HOPR tokens to fund the channel with.

FundChannelResponse.description: Response body for funding a channel.

MultiaddressSource.description: A multiaddress paired with its discovery origin.

NodeChannel.description: Channel information as seen by the node.

all.description: Complete channel topology as seen by this node.

NodeInfoResponse.description:
Information about the current node. Covers network, addresses, eligibility, connectivity status, contracts addresses
and indexer state.

NodePeerInfoResponse.description: Comprehensive information about a peer: multiaddresses, QoS observations, and channel state.

announcedSources.description: Announced multiaddresses grouped by discovery origin.

oneOf.description: Incoming channel (peer → this node), if one exists and is not closed.
Outgoing channel (this node → peer), if one exists and is not closed.
QoS observation data from the network graph, if connected.

OpenChannelBodyRequest.description: Request body for opening a channel.

amount.description: Initial amount of stake in HOPR tokens.

OpenChannelResponse.description: Response body for opening a channel.

transactionReceipt.description: Receipt of the channel open transaction.

PeerChannelInfo.description: Channel information for a specific peer.

PeerQosInfo.description: QoS observation data for a peer.

averageLatency.description: Average latency in milliseconds, if available.

lastUpdate.description: Epoch milliseconds of the last observation update.

PingResponse.description: Contains the latency and the reported version of a peer that has been pinged.

RedeemTicketsRequest.description: Request body for ticket redemption with optional fields.

address.description:
On-chain address of the counterparty whose incoming channel tickets to redeem.
If omitted, tickets in all channels are redeemed.

SessionCapability.description: Session capabilities that can be negotiated with the target peer.

SessionClientRequest.description: Request body for creating a new client session.

capabilities.description:
Capabilities for the Session protocol.

Defaults to `Segmentation` and `Retransmission` for TCP and nothing for UDP.

forwardPath.description: The forward path for the Session.

listenHost.description:
Listen host (`ip:port`) for the Session socket at the Entry node.

Supports also partial specification (only `ip` or only `:port`) with the
respective part replaced by the node's configured default.

maxClientSessions.description:
The maximum number of client sessions that the listener can spawn.

This currently applies only to the TCP sessions, as UDP sessions cannot
handle multiple clients (and spawn therefore always only a single session).

If this value is smaller than the value specified in `session_pool`, it will
be set to that value.

The default value is 5.

maxSurbUpstream.description:
The maximum throughput at which artificial SURBs might be generated and sent
to the recipient of the Session.

On Sessions that rarely send data but receive a lot (= Exit node has high SURB consumption),
this should roughly match the maximum retrieval throughput.

All syntaxes like "2 MBps", "1.2Mbps", "300 kb/s", "1.23 Mb/s" are supported.

responseBuffer.description:
The amount of response data the Session counterparty can deliver back to us,
without us sending any SURBs to them.

In other words, this size is recalculated to a number of SURBs delivered
to the counterparty upfront and then maintained.
The maintenance is dynamic, based on the number of responses we receive.

All syntaxes like "2 MB", "128 kiB", "3MiB" are supported. The value must be
at least the size of 2 Session packet payloads.

returnPath.description: The return path for the Session.

sessionPool.description:
How many Sessions to pool for clients.

If no sessions are pooled, they will be opened ad-hoc when a client connects.
It has no effect on UDP sessions in the current implementation.

Currently, the maximum value is 5.

SessionClientResponse.description: Response body for creating a new client session.

activeClients.description:
Lists Session IDs of all active clients.

Can contain multiple entries on TCP sessions, but currently
always only a single entry on UDP sessions.

destination.description: Destination node (exit node) of the Session.

hoprMtu.description: MTU used by the underlying HOPR transport.

ip.description: Listening IP address of the Session's socket.

maxClientSessions.description:
The maximum number of client sessions that the listener can spawn.

This currently applies only to the TCP sessions, as UDP sessions cannot
have multiple clients (defaults to 1 for UDP).

maxSurbUpstream.description:
The maximum throughput at which artificial SURBs might be generated and sent
to the recipient of the Session.

port.description: Listening port of the Session's socket.

protocol.description: IP protocol used by Session's listening socket.

responseBuffer.description:
The amount of response data the Session counterparty can deliver back to us, without us
sending any SURBs to them.

sessionPool.description: How many Sessions to pool for clients.

surbLen.description:
Size of a Single Use Reply Block used by the protocol.

This is useful for SURB balancing calculations.

maxSurbUpstream.description:
The maximum throughput at which artificial SURBs might be generated and sent
to the recipient of the Session.

On Sessions that rarely send data but receive a lot (= Exit node has high SURB consumption),
this should roughly match the maximum retrieval throughput.

All syntaxes like "2 MBps", "1.2Mbps", "300 kb/s", "1.23 Mb/s" are supported.

responseBuffer.description:
The amount of response data the Session counterparty can deliver back to us,
without us sending any SURBs to them.

In other words, this size is recalculated to a number of SURBs delivered
to the counterparty upfront and then maintained.
The maintenance is dynamic, based on the number of responses we receive.

All syntaxes like "2 MB", "128 kiB", "3MiB" are supported. The value must be
at least the size of 2 Session packet payloads.

TicketPriceResponse.description: Contains the ticket price in HOPR tokens.

price.description: Price of the ticket in HOPR tokens.

TicketProbabilityResponse.description: Contains the winning probability of a ticket.

WithdrawBodyRequest.description: Request body for the withdrawal endpoint.

WithdrawResponse.description: Response body for the withdrawal endpoint.
